narrative: OMX30 opens to broad-based global tailwinds with DAX, CAC and NASDAQ all substantially higher overnight, generating a +1.96% weighted correlation signal. The yield environment has shifted notably lower (US 10Y down 174bp), which should ease DCF compression fears and support equity valuations, though the 40bps move in rates requires careful positioning. Technicals remain supportive—the index sits +7.6% above its 200-day moving average in confirmed bull regime—but RSI at 42.46 and MACD below signal line suggest consolidation or modest retracement risk. The ADX strength (25.8) with bearish DI bias indicates profit-taking pressure despite positive sentiment. Commodity strength (gold +3.76%, copper +2.93%) provides tailwind for Boliden and mining exposure, while weaker SEK (EUR/SEK -0.65%, USD/SEK -1.08%) benefits industrial exporters.
morningBrief: OMX30 expected to open higher on the back of strong European (DAX +1.97%, CAC +2.32%) and NASDAQ (+2.54%) rallies overnight, plus sharp yield compression in US 10Y (-174bp to 4.463%). Watch the 3180 level (50-MA) as near-term resistance; support sits at 3085 (lower Bollinger). ADX shows strong technicals but with bearish directional bias, so anticipate some profit-taking despite the positive correlation backdrop. Sector focus: Boliden a clear long on gold/copper strength; Banks (SEB, Handelsbanken, Swedbank) benefit from marginally lower but still supportive rate environment; Industrials (Atlas Copco, Volvo B) supported by weaker SEK (USD/SEK -1.08%) but watch for oil weakness drag (-4.34%) on input cost narratives. Avoid oversizing tech on NASDAQ strength given RSI neutral and MACD below signal—mean reversion risk remains. Watch US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET for any surprises that could reverse the yield move.
drivers: Strong global equity momentum (DAX +1.97%, NASDAQ +2.54%, Nikkei +2.81%) with weighted correlation +1.96%
Gold rally +3.76% supports mining sector; copper +2.93% strongly positive for Boliden
Yield compression (US 10Y -1.74% to 4.463%) reduces refinancing pressure and supports equity multiples
OMX30 structural strength: +7.6% above 200-MA, in bull regime, although ADX 25.8 shows strong bearish DI bias

sectorImpacts.reason: Gold +3.76% and copper +2.93% provide direct revenue uplift; both commodities rallying on risk-on sentiment and yield compression.
US 10Y yield compression (-174bp) reduces immediate refinancing pain, and 4.463% level still supports NIM environment for Swedish banks.
Weaker SEK (USD/SEK -1.08%, EUR/SEK -0.65%) boosts export competitiveness; offset partially by Brent oil weakness (-4.34%) on input cost concerns.
NASDAQ +2.54% supports sentiment, but RSI 42.46 neutral and MACD below signal suggest caution; yield compression is mixed (DCF benefit vs. rate-sensitive rotation risk).
Weaker EUR/SEK (-0.65%) raises sourcing costs for H&M; oil weakness provides minimal offset to margin pressure.
Yield compression is beneficial longer-term, but current 4.463% US level and SEK stability limit near-term re-rating. Monitor capital raise refinancing windows.

watchList.reason: Gold +3.76% and copper +2.93% create optimal environment. Direct commodity beta play with strong momentum.
watchList.catalyst: Commodity rally continuation; earnings leverage to metals prices

watchList.reason: Dual benefit: weaker SEK supports export margins (USD/SEK -1.08%) and industrial export demand remains elevated on global equity strength.
watchList.catalyst: Earnings revisions on FX benefit; order intake trends

watchList.reason: Premium industrial exporter with 28% weighting in OMX30; benefits from USD/SEK weakness and risk-on industrial sentiment.
NASDAQ strength overnight is positive, but RSI neutral and MACD below signal line argue against aggressive chasing. Wait for technical confirmation.
watchList.catalyst: MACD crossover above signal; RSI above 50

watchList.reason: Yield compression provides relief from prior refinancing fears. NIM still supportive at 4.463% US 10Y. Monitor for any dovish pivot signals.
watchList.catalyst: Q2 earnings NIM guidance; ECB/Swedish Riksbank commentary

watchList.reason: EUR/SEK weakness (-0.65%) creates sourcing headwinds. Oil weakness does not offset cost pressure. Wait for stabilization or technical support.

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish economic releases scheduled. US Jobless Claims (Initial + Continuing) at 14:30 CET is key risk event—watch for surprise widening that could reverse yield compression. Potential for intra-day volatility if claims miss expectations.
topRisk: Yield reversion higher if US labor data disappoints or Fed guidance shifts hawkish; ADX strength with bearish DI bias suggests profit-taking pull-back despite positive correlation backdrop; SpaceX IPO noise (Musk headlines) adds sentiment volatility.
technicalSummary: RSI 42.46 neutral; no overbought conditions, mean reversion risk contained
MACD below signal line (-5.705 histogram) — minor caution, but not breakdown signal yet
ADX 25.8 strong but bearish DI bias — suggests profit-taking in early moves
Bollinger %B=0.64 in upper half, width 5.06% moderate volatility; not stretched
Stochastic K=69.4 above D=33.2 — slight bullish divergence, but neutral overall
